label: fiction
title: Locker Seventy-Two
setting: A parcel-locker possession trial in 2026, where a recycled phone number directs police to the wrong subscriber.
charge: Knowingly possessing stolen prescription medicine for sale
elements: The accused performed or participated in the alleged act.
The act was deliberate or dishonest rather than accidental.
The prosecution has proved every required fact beyond reasonable doubt.
hook: A locker opens from a text message. Police find stolen medicine inside. The phone number has belonged to two people in one month.

accused.human: Ilya Morn works rotating shifts and spends spare evenings translating forms for newly arrived neighbours.
accused.if_guilty: A conviction means prison, loss of housing tied to employment, and the collapse of informal support many neighbours rely upon.

prosecution.speaker: pc
prosecution.text: The prosecution says the accused received the collection code, approached the locker, and lied about why he was standing nearby. The apparent centre of the case is a locker code sent to the accused’s number, but the evidence must be followed through the complete sequence rather than treated as a single dramatic clue.

defence.speaker: dc
defence.text: The defence says the locker booking belonged to the number’s previous subscriber and the accused was collecting an unrelated parcel from the next bank. Suspicion gathered around a person before investigators tested the limits of a locker code sent to the accused’s number; those limits matter because a criminal verdict cannot rest on an attractive shortcut.

prosecution.speaker: pc
prosecution.text: The prosecution began with the locker code associated with Ilya’s current phone number, but it does not ask you to convict on that clue alone. Ilya stood at the locker bank and later saw a message connected with the stolen medicine compartment. The defence has exposed real limits in the first impression; those limits deserve weight, not automatic control of the verdict. Consider the records together, apply the judge’s direction to every element, and return guilty only if this connected evidence leaves you sure beyond reasonable doubt.

defence.speaker: dc
defence.text: The defence accepts that the locker code associated with Ilya’s current phone number created suspicion, but suspicion is not the test. The number was assigned to Ilya six days after booking, and camera records show him opening locker seventy-three with his own order. That evidence goes to the heart of the case because a current-subscriber search cannot identify who held the number when the locker was booked. The accused need not identify another offender. If this competing account remains reasonably possible, the only lawful verdict is not guilty.

epilogue: Ilya returned to work after the employer reviewed the acquittal. The locker operator began storing subscriber assignment dates with warrants, and investigators identified the prior number holder through an older delivery account. The people involved kept living with the consequences after the courtroom emptied. The case changed workplace rules, but it could not return the months spent under accusation.

beats.speaker: pc
beats.text: Police recovered sealed stolen medicine from locker seventy-two. The collection code had been sent to the mobile number investigators then associated with Ilya. At this first point, the exhibit’s stated limit is that a current-subscriber search cannot identify who held the number when the locker was booked, so it must be weighed with the rest of the sequence.
beats.reveal_note: The first impression came from the locker code associated with Ilya’s current phone number; its value depended on whether the later evidence could overcome this limit: a current-subscriber search cannot identify who held the number when the locker was booked.

beats.speaker: w2
beats.text: Camera footage shows Ilya entering the locker hall at 6:18. A pillar blocks his hands, and the original report assumed he opened seventy-two because that door moved soon afterwards. This was the second fact I recorded in the initial Locker Seventy-Two inquiry, before investigators settled on Ilya Morn. The timing was noted before the Locker Seventy-Two inquiry settled on Ilya Morn as its suspect.
beats.reveal_note: This witness fixed the practical setting before suspicion centred on Ilya Morn, giving the room context for the records that followed.

beats.speaker: w1
beats.text: You did not obtain carrier assignment dates before arrest? No. We relied on the current subscriber return. That return said nothing about who held the number at booking? It did not include historical assignment dates. The arrest relied on a current-subscriber return that contained no historical assignment date.

turns.speaker: dc
turns.text: You did not obtain carrier assignment dates before arrest?

turns.speaker: w1
turns.text: No. We relied on the current subscriber return.

turns.speaker: dc
turns.text: That return said nothing about who held the number at booking?

turns.speaker: w1
turns.text: It did not include historical assignment dates. The arrest relied on a current-subscriber return that contained no historical assignment date.

beats.reveal_note: The challenged record mattered because it exposed the gap between appearance and proof: a current-subscriber search cannot identify who held the number when the locker was booked.

beats.speaker: pc
beats.text: The locker service stored the phone number but not the subscriber name. A later customer search returned Ilya because he held the number when investigators asked. At this fourth point, the exhibit’s stated limit is that a current-subscriber search cannot identify who held the number when the locker was booked, so it must be weighed with the rest of the sequence.
beats.reveal_note: Cross-examination narrowed the early clue without erasing it, forcing the room to decide how much independent support remained.

beats.speaker: dc
beats.text: Carrier assignment records show the number moved to Ilya six days after locker seventy-two was booked and two days after its first collection reminder. At this fifth point, the exhibit’s stated limit is that a current-subscriber search cannot identify who held the number when the locker was booked, so it must be weighed with the rest of the sequence.
beats.reveal_note: This was the decisive innocence evidence: Carrier assignment records show the number moved to Ilya six days after locker seventy-two was booked and two days after its first collection reminder. It turned the alternative explanation into a checkable account the prosecution could not exclude beyond reasonable doubt.

beats.speaker: w3
beats.text: A second camera shows Ilya carrying a parcel whose label matches locker seventy-three. Its legitimate collection record opens within the same minute using his email address. This was the sixth fact I recorded in the initial Locker Seventy-Two inquiry, before investigators settled on Ilya Morn. In the Locker Seventy-Two inquiry, I separated what I personally observed from what investigators later asked me to infer.
beats.reveal_note: The competing account was concrete rather than speculative: the number was assigned to Ilya six days after booking, and camera records show him opening locker seventy-three with his own order.

beats.speaker: w4
beats.text: I said I was collecting work boots because that is what I ordered. I did not know medicine was beside me, and I never received the earlier locker reminders. This was the seventh fact I recorded in the initial Locker Seventy-Two inquiry, before investigators settled on Ilya Morn. That Locker Seventy-Two detail can be checked against the contemporaneous record rather than accepted as a later reconstruction.
beats.reveal_note: This testimony tested whether the system record described a person’s conduct or only an event produced around them.

beats.speaker: w2
beats.text: Your system recycled a customer search? It searched the number as it existed when police asked. So it attached a later subscriber to an earlier booking? Yes. That is what happened in this case. The service attached a later subscriber to an earlier booking; that database join created the apparent identification.

turns.speaker: dc
turns.text: Your system recycled a customer search?

turns.speaker: w2
turns.text: It searched the number as it existed when police asked.

turns.speaker: dc
turns.text: So it attached a later subscriber to an earlier booking?

turns.speaker: w2
turns.text: Yes. That is what happened in this case. The service attached a later subscriber to an earlier booking; that database join created the apparent identification.

beats.reveal_note: The second cross put the decisive inference under pressure and showed exactly which part of the account could still be disputed.

beats.speaker: w4
beats.text: You received one final code? I saw a code after police had already contacted me. You never tried it at locker seventy-two? No. I had my own code and opened locker seventy-three. A late message seen after police contact does not show that Ilya received or used the original collection code.

turns.speaker: w4
turns.text: I saw a code after police had already contacted me.

turns.speaker: pc
turns.text: You never tried it at locker seventy-two?

turns.speaker: w4
turns.text: No. I had my own code and opened locker seventy-three. A late message seen after police contact does not show that Ilya received or used the original collection code.

beats.reveal_note: The final witness supplied a separate check on the timeline, reducing the risk that one attractive clue would decide the case alone.

beats.speaker: judge
beats.text: The prosecution must prove every element beyond reasonable doubt. The accused does not have to prove an alternative account, explain every suspicious detail, or show who else was responsible. Treat digital records like any other evidence: examine their origin, limits, and connection to a person.
beats.reveal_note: The direction kept the burden where it belonged: a current-subscriber search cannot identify who held the number when the locker was booked, and no gap in the defence could substitute for proof of every element.

twist: The locker service searched by current subscriber and made Ilya look like the recipient. Carrier records showed the number was assigned to him six days after the medicine locker was booked, while camera timing placed him opening a different compartment.
